draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users want metrics-first, scannable overviews rather than narrative explanations or dense data tables
facade.axisTargeted: Density — key metrics vs. comprehensive data
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward prominent KPIs, card-based layouts, and at-a-glance dashboards for workspace status
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward detailed documentation, contextual explanations, tables with full datasets, or narrative-driven interfaces

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer subtle, diffused lighting over harsh contrast
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Preference for gentle transitions, calming depth, reduced eye strain — likely values comfort in focus-heavy work
facade.rejectImplies: Preference for high contrast, crisp clarity, bold definition — likely values clarity and visual hierarchy over ambient softness

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer conversational, approachable language over corporate formality
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, everyday vocabulary; use contractions; encourage a sense of ease and collaboration
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, formal language; emphasize expertise and professional boundaries; use structured, measured tone

evidence.hypothesis: AI workspace users want metrics-first, scannable overviews rather than narrative explanations or dense data tables
evidence.implication: Lean toward prominent KPIs, card-based layouts, and at-a-glance dashboards for workspace status

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces expect snappy, zero-lag interactions over smooth transitions—clicks should feel immediate rather than animated
facade.label: Instant responses
facade.acceptImplies: Prioritize responsiveness and direct feedback; minimize decorative motion; make every interaction feel like it completes instantly
facade.rejectImplies: Motion and transitions matter; users value the journey between states; animations should feel smooth and purposeful rather than getting out of the way

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer a persistent left navigation paired with content cards rather than tabs or lists, because it balances quick access to tools with focused, modular content areas.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Future probes should explore sidebar variations (width, collapsibility, icon-only states) and card density/spacing.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab-based navigation or full-width list layouts as primary navigation patterns instead.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer warm, diffused illumination over harsh digital brightness—signaling a human-centered rather than purely utilitarian approach.
facade.label: Soft ambient lighting
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace aesthetic should feel inviting and less sterile; prioritize visual comfort and organic lighting metaphors in UI/UX decisions.
facade.rejectImplies: Users favor clarity and high contrast over atmospheric mood; the workspace should emphasize precision, clean interfaces, and digital-native aesthetics.

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer chronological narrative feeds over static dashboard snapshots, wanting to see what changed and when rather than just current state
facade.label: Scrollable activity feed
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into timeline-based interfaces, event logs, and 'what just happened' storytelling—even if it means less scannable at a glance
facade.rejectImplies: Reinforce metrics-first, static card layouts with minimal historical context—users want speed over story

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and coral accents.
